top_entities.name: Anthropic
top_entities.news_summary: EXPORT CONTROL DAY 13-25 (Jun 11-25): Congressional June 26 deadline for Commerce/Lutnick response arrives TOMORROW. Four bipartisan members sent Jun 18 letter demanding written explanation of Fable 5/Mythos 5 export controls. Anthropic publicly disputed proportionality of jailbreak-triggered recall. First-ever export control applied to commercially-deployed AI model. EU AI Act enforcement powers activate Aug 2 (38 days). Claude Fable 5/Mythos 5 remain suspended globally. Composite 60.0 — exactly at functional/established boundary (60). Pending commercial-impact governance question unresolved.

top_entities.name: Sudan
top_entities.news_summary: EL OBEID CRITICAL — NEW DAY-COUNT (Jun 11-25): Ten consecutive days of RSF drone strikes on El Obeid/North Kordofan killing 50+ civilians. UN rights chief warning of imminent atrocities; 500,000+ displaced to El Obeid. At least 29 countries issued UNHRC atrocity warning Jun 18. Famine declared in El-Fasher and Kadugli; 20 additional areas at risk. OCHA Jun 12 sitrep: humanitarian access near-impossible. US State Dept statement active. Jun 24: UN expert said city 'weeks from catastrophe.'

top_entities.name: xAI/Grok
top_entities.news_summary: DUAL ACTIVE LEGAL (Jun 11-25): (1) OPC Canada PIPEDA ruling Jun 11: X Corp./xAI violated privacy law — Grok Imagine generated 6,000+ non-consensual sexualized deepfakes/hour targeting women and children; privacy impact assessment not completed until March 2026, months after Jul 2025 launch; 'did not accurately reflect' safety risks. (2) Devin Kim whistleblower lawsuit active in Santa Clara Superior Court — fired before safety presentation; first AI safety whistleblower retaliation case. SpaceX IPO on Nasdaq (Jun 12, $1.75T). Composite 17.5 — deep critical band.

top_entities.name: OpenAI
top_entities.news_summary: 42-STATE AG PROBE ACTIVE (Jun 11-25): Jun 13 multistate subpoena led by NY AG Letitia James — ads, data, minors, seniors, health data, model sycophancy, internal safety policies. Florida criminal investigation (ChatGPT/FSU shooting link) active since Apr; FL AG filed first-in-nation state lawsuit Jun 1 naming CEO Sam Altman personally. IPO filing (confidential SEC) 5 days before subpoena. Broadest consumer-protection action ever against frontier AI lab. Composite 27.5 — developing band.

top_entities.name: Iran
top_entities.news_summary: EXECUTION SURGE ESCALATING (Jun 11-25): Jun 13-16 alone: 31 prisoners executed — one every three hours. Khordad month total: 134 executions. 784+ total YTD — highest rate in 37 years. 45+ political executions YTD. Jun 9 NCRI brief documents ongoing spree. Prison hunger strike now 120+ consecutive weeks across 56 prisons. US-Iran talks ongoing while executions accelerate. UN: 18 protesters executed in 2026 with 78 at imminent risk. Iran executing political prisoners while conducting US peace talks per WSJ.

top_entities.name: Democratic Republic of Congo
top_entities.news_summary: DUAL CRITICAL ESCALATING (Jun 11-25): Ebola: Jun 22 = 1,094 cases/277 deaths — 2nd-largest outbreak ever; Bundibugyo strain (no available vaccine). WHO Jun 19: outbreak accelerating beyond borders into Uganda. 37 days to reach 250 deaths vs 78 days in 2014 W.Africa. PHEIC since May 16. M23/Rwanda: HRW Jun 10 'Death Was Everywhere' — Washington Agreement unimplemented; ceasefire repeatedly violated; 2,100+ killed since Jun 2025 peace deal. Contact tracing at 45% vs 90% needed due to ongoing conflict.

top_entities.name: Microsoft
top_entities.news_summary: AZURE/ISRAEL INVESTIGATION CLOSED (Jun 11-25): Jun 4 investigation summary published via Covington & Burling review — confirmed IMOD used Azure storage (Netherlands) and AI services; 200M hours of Palestinian surveillance recordings stored. Microsoft Israel GM ousted. New governance: stricter upfront security-agency deal reviews; conflict-zone policy review; tightened human rights controls. Microsoft France now manages Israel operations. $9.7B Dept of War contract (Jun 1) raises continued military-AI governance questions. EU AI Act enforcement Aug 2.

top_entities.name: UnitedHealth Group
top_entities.news_summary: DOJ CRIMINAL + ANTITRUST PROBE ACTIVE (Jun 11-25): Criminal and civil inquiries into Medicare Advantage overbilling (inflated diagnoses via in-home nurse assessments); Optum Rx billing; physician reimbursement. DOJ antitrust probe of Optum/insurance competitive relationship. UNH disclosed in SEC filing; launched third-party billing review. Probe expanded beyond Medicare Advantage. CMS also scrutinizing. DOJ Jun 23 healthcare fraud takedown (455 defendants, $6.5B) puts sector-wide spotlight on UNH practices. Composite near band boundary.

top_entities.name: Israel
top_entities.news_summary: DUAL-FRONT ONGOING (Jun 11-25): Gaza: 72,996+ killed (Jun 14), 392 UNRWA staff killed. UNRWA blocked from entering since Jan 2025; aid average 145 trucks/day vs 600 agreed. Jun 17 ceasefire — 981 additional Palestinian deaths since ceasefire (Oct 2025). Lebanon: Jun 19 US-brokered truce (Hezbollah/Israel) but fighting continues; Jun 9 eight killed in Tyre; Jun 13 five killed incl. mayor; Amnesty Jun report: Israel 'radically expanded unlawful mass evacuation orders — war crime of unlawful transfer.' Displacement orders covering 29 towns in southern Lebanon.

top_entities.name: Apple
top_entities.news_summary: TOWSON NLRB ACTIVE (Jun 11-25): Apple closed its first-ever unionized store (Towson, MD, IAM Local 4538) Jun 20. IAM filed Unfair Labor Practice charge: Towson workers not offered same relocation as non-union Trumbull/Escondido closures; required to reapply. 54-member Congressional Labor Caucus called for NLRB investigation. Workers testified at Capitol Hill NLRB hearing. Apple protest #DoRightApple rally May 27. Case ongoing — NLRB complaint potential.

top_entities.name: Somalia
top_entities.news_summary: FAMINE IMMINENT (Jun 11-25): Buur Hakaba district famine projected by end of June; credible risk in Bay, Bakool, Gedo through September. 6M face life-threatening food insecurity (1-in-3 Somalis). 1.9M children acutely malnourished. WFP reaching only 1-in-10 people needing food — dramatic drop from last year. Al-Shabaab taxing water/food/livestock; destroying food deliveries. Food prices up 70% in some areas; fuel up 150% due to Hormuz. Somalia Barrels toward Famine per Crisis Group.

top_entities.name: Nigeria
top_entities.news_summary: ONGOING CRITICAL CONFIRMED (Jun 11-25): 35M at IPC Phase 3+ lean season (Jun-Aug) — highest ever recorded in Nigeria. 15,000 at Phase 5 Catastrophe in Borno. JNIM (al-Qaeda affiliate) first-ever Nigeria attack confirmed; ISWAP expanding across Sahel. WFP: imminent funding cuts imminent. FAO-WFP: Nigeria highest-concern hotspot globally. Jun 19 UN brief: fighting driving Crisis-Emergency outcomes expanding Jun-Sep. Conflict proximate in Borno, Adamawa, Yobe.

top_entities.name: Burkina Faso
top_entities.news_summary: ONGOING CRITICAL (Jun 11-25): IPS Jun 10: 'Violence, Climate Shocks, and Hunger Push The Sahel To Brink of Collapse.' 52.8M face acute food insecurity in West Africa/Sahel lean season. UN CERD findings: Fulani subjected to extrajudicial killings, enforced disappearances, arbitrary detention by state and non-state actors. 2M+ IDPs in Burkina Faso. 3.5M+ trapped in besieged areas. UN Special Procedures: systematic enforced disappearances at crimes-against-humanity threshold. ICRC: drone strikes killing civilians may constitute war crimes.

top_entities.name: Mali
top_entities.news_summary: ONGOING CRITICAL + OPEN CALIBRATION QUESTION (Jun 11-25): UN Special Procedures: systematic enforced disappearances in Mali at crimes-against-humanity threshold confirmed in Feb 2026 — still active. Three political opposition leaders/junta critics disappeared May 8-11 Bamako. Crisis-hunger population surged 64% since 2023; 1.5M severely food insecure. WFP rations reduced. JNIM expanding from Mali into Nigeria — first cross-border attack. Open calibration question from prior cycle unresolved: current composite may not reflect crimes-against-humanity evidence threshold.

top_entities.name: Humana
top_entities.news_summary: DOJ FCA/BARROWS ACTIVE (Jun 11-25): DOJ FCA complaint active against Humana for kickbacks and discrimination against disabled Americans (filed separately from $90M Part D settlement). Barrows class action (nH Predict AI algorithm denying nursing facility claims) ongoing — 86-year-old plaintiff. DOJ Jun 23 healthcare fraud takedown sector pressure: 455 defendants, $6.5B. Humana recently assessed Jun 23 — reassessment appropriate given sector-wide fraud action and continued open FCA complaint. Composite 35.2 near developing/functional boundary (40).

sector_alerts.title: Anthropic Commerce export-control deadline: Congressional Jun 26 response due TOMORROW
sector_alerts.summary: Four bipartisan Congress members demanded written explanation from Commerce Sec. Lutnick by Jun 26 regarding Fable 5/Mythos 5 export controls. Deadline is tomorrow. Response (or non-response) will materially affect assessment of Anthropic's governance position and the broader AI export-control precedent. If Commerce does not respond, Congressional escalation likely. Fable/Mythos remain globally suspended — Day 13 as of Jun 25.

sector_alerts.title: Sudan El Obeid — RSF drone campaign ongoing; 29-country atrocity warning; city 'weeks from catastrophe'
sector_alerts.summary: RSF drone strikes on El Obeid entering second week (Jun 11-25). 50+ civilians killed by drone strikes. UN human rights chief warning of 'impending atrocities.' 29+ countries issued UNHRC atrocity-prevention warning Jun 18. Famine declared in El-Fasher and Kadugli; 20 additional areas at risk. 500,000+ people sheltering in El Obeid from displaced areas. US State Dept issued atrocity-threat statement.

sector_alerts.title: DRC Ebola — 1,094 cases/277 deaths Jun 22; accelerating beyond borders; fastest-growing outbreak in history
sector_alerts.summary: Jun 22 WHO: 1,094 confirmed cases, 277 deaths. Bundibugyo strain — no approved vaccine. Accelerating beyond DRC borders into Uganda. 37 days to 250 deaths vs 78 days in 2014 West Africa outbreak. PHEIC since May 16. Contact tracing at 45% vs 90% required due to M23 conflict in eastern DRC.

sector_alerts.title: EU AI Act enforcement powers activate Aug 2, 2026 — 38 days; GPAI fines now possible for non-compliance
sector_alerts.summary: Aug 2, 2026 = European Commission's enforcement powers fully activate for GPAI models. Max penalty: €15M or 3% global turnover. AI Office gains full investigatory authority: documentation requests, model access, on-site inspections, corrective measures. All major AI labs (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google DeepMind, Meta) subject to compliance with GPAI obligations that have been in force since Aug 2, 2025. This is a 38-day countdown for the sector.

sector_alerts.title: DOJ National Healthcare Fraud Takedown — 455 defendants, $6.5B; sector-wide enforcement signal
sector_alerts.summary: Jun 23: DOJ announced 2026 National Health Care Fraud Takedown — 455 defendants, 90 doctors and licensed professionals, $6.5B in false claims. 56 federal districts, 45 states. CMS suspended 1,079 providers. Cases span home health, hospice, wound care, telehealth, behavioral health. Several allegations involve patient harm including death. Creates elevated scrutiny context for UnitedHealth Group, Humana, CVS Health, and other large healthcare Fortune 500 entities.

sector_alerts.title: Somalia famine imminent — Buur Hakaba by end of June; WFP reaching 1-in-10 in need
sector_alerts.summary: Crisis Group: Somalia barrels toward famine amid aid cuts. IPC famine projected for Buur Hakaba district by end of June 2026. Credible risk in Bay, Bakool, Gedo through September. WFP reaching only 1-in-10 people needing food. Al-Shabaab food blockade and taxation escalating. 1.9M children acutely malnourished. Funding crisis acute.

sector_alerts.title: Iran execution surge — 31 executed in 4 days Jun 13-16; YTD 784; highest rate in 37 years
sector_alerts.summary: Iran executing political prisoners at rate not seen since 1988. 134 executions in Khordad (May 22-Jun 21). 31 in 4-day span Jun 13-16. Jun 25 NCRI brief documents ongoing killing. 45+ political executions YTD. Execution pace intensifying while Iran conducts US nuclear peace talks. Prison hunger strike 120+ consecutive weeks at 56 prisons.

sector_alerts.title: Fortune 500 DEI disclosures fell 65% in 2026 — sector-wide governance signal
sector_alerts.summary: Human Rights Campaign Foundation: Fortune 500 DEI disclosure participation dropped 65% year-over-year in 2026 — from 377 to 131 firms. Political/regulatory environment cited as driver. This is a systemic governance-transparency signal affecting assessments of Fortune 500 labor/equity subdimensions across the index.

entity_reviews.name: Ukraine
entity_reviews.summary: ONGOING CRITICAL (Jun 11-25): Russian Jun 2 attack killed 22, wounded 138 across 38 locations. Daily strikes continue. OHCHR May 31: 62,716 civilian casualties (16,126 killed). Special Tribunal for crime of aggression active. Air defense gaps due to depleted US stocks from Iran war.

entity_reviews.name: Russia
entity_reviews.summary: ONGOING CRITICAL (Jun 11-25): Daily strikes on Ukraine. 1,700+ Ukrainians arbitrarily detained incommunicado; torture systematic. 210,000+ alleged war crimes. ICC prosecutor intimidation documented. Special Tribunal active.

entity_reviews.name: Israel
entity_reviews.summary: DUAL-FRONT ONGOING (Jun 11-25): Gaza 72,996+ killed. UNRWA blocked. Lebanon: Jun 19 truce; fighting continues; Amnesty: unlawful mass evacuation orders = war crime of unlawful transfer.

entity_reviews.name: Lebanon
entity_reviews.summary: ONGOING CRITICAL (Jun 11-25): Jun 19 US-brokered truce agreed but fighting continued — Jun 9 eight killed in Tyre, Jun 13 five killed incl. mayor of Ar-Rihan. Amnesty: Israel 'radically expanded unlawful mass evacuation orders'; 29 towns displacement orders issued. 4,000+ killed, 1.2M+ displaced since Mar 2 war start.

entity_reviews.name: Iran
entity_reviews.summary: EXECUTION SURGE ESCALATING (Jun 11-25): 31 executed Jun 13-16 (one every 3 hours). 134 in Khordad. 784+ YTD — 37-year high. Jun 25 NCRI brief documents ongoing. 45+ political executions. Hunger strikes 120+ weeks at 56 prisons. Iran executing prisoners during US peace talks.

entity_reviews.name: Sudan
entity_reviews.summary: EL OBEID CRITICAL (Jun 11-25): 10 consecutive days of RSF drone strikes on El Obeid, 50+ civilian deaths. 29-country UNHRC atrocity warning Jun 18. Famine in El-Fasher and Kadugli. UN: city weeks from catastrophe.

entity_reviews.name: South Sudan
entity_reviews.summary: ONGOING CRITICAL (Jun 11-25): 140,000 displaced in Akobo County alone; 300,000+ across Jonglei. 10M need humanitarian assistance (2/3 of population). WFP serving 1.5M fewer than planned. Aid workers attacked, bureaucratic obstruction widespread.

entity_reviews.name: Yemen
entity_reviews.summary: ONGOING CRITICAL (Jun 11-25): 73 UN workers still detained by Houthis; one died in custody. 22.3M need humanitarian assistance. Only 12.7% of $2.16B funding met. Aid assets seized, access severely restricted. HRW Jun 7 called for release of UN/civil society staff.

entity_reviews.name: Somalia
entity_reviews.summary: FAMINE IMMINENT (Jun 11-25): Buur Hakaba famine projected end of June. 6M face life-threatening food insecurity. WFP reaching only 1-in-10. Al-Shabaab food blockades escalating. 1.9M children malnourished.

entity_reviews.name: Nigeria
entity_reviews.summary: ONGOING CRITICAL (Jun 11-25): 35M at IPC Phase 3+; 15,000 at Phase 5 Catastrophe in Borno. JNIM first Nigeria attack. ISWAP expanding. WFP funding cuts imminent.

entity_reviews.name: Burkina Faso
entity_reviews.summary: ONGOING CRITICAL (Jun 11-25): UN CERD findings: Fulani subjected to extrajudicial killings, enforced disappearances. 2M+ IDPs. Drone strikes on civilians. IPS Jun 10: Sahel on 'brink of collapse.'

entity_reviews.name: Mali
entity_reviews.summary: ONGOING CRITICAL (Jun 11-25): Three opposition figures disappeared May 8-11 Bamako. UN Special Procedures: systematic enforced disappearances at crimes-against-humanity threshold. 1.5M severely food insecure. JNIM expanding into Nigeria.

entity_reviews.name: Democratic Republic of Congo
entity_reviews.summary: DUAL CRITICAL (Jun 11-25): Ebola 1,094 cases/277 deaths Jun 22; accelerating beyond borders. Washington Agreement unimplemented; 2,100+ killed since Jun 2025 peace deal. HRW Jun 17: 'Trump declared peace in Congo — this is the reality.'

entity_reviews.name: Afghanistan
entity_reviews.summary: ONGOING CRITICAL (Jun 11-25): UN Jun: 'life becoming impossible in remote areas.' 22M in need; UN $1.7B appeal only 14% funded. Women civil servants terminated without pay Jan 2026. Only country banning girls from secondary/higher education. 230+ Taliban decrees criminalizing women's rights.

entity_reviews.name: Myanmar
entity_reviews.summary: ONGOING CRITICAL (Jun 11-25): Junta controls <40% of townships. 3.6M+ internally displaced; 15M+ food insecure. Arakan Army (controls most of Rakhine) ramping oppressive measures vs Rohingya. Junta enforces aid blockades. Fraudulent elections Jan 2026 dismissed internationally.

entity_reviews.name: Ethiopia
entity_reviews.summary: ONGOING WATCH (Jun 11-25): Renewed Tigray/Amhara border fighting Jan 2026; IDPs protesting in Tigray capital. Aid access restricted in Amhara (kidnappings). Funding cuts worsening gap. US aid cuts devastating Tigray per Al Jazeera Jan 23.

entity_reviews.name: North Korea
entity_reviews.summary: ONGOING WATCH (Jun 11-25): Kim Yo Jong Jun statements on irreversible nuclear status. IAEA Apr 2026: 'very serious' nuclear advances; 'few dozen' warheads. HRW Mar: rights crisis needs global attention alongside missile focus.

entity_reviews.name: China
entity_reviews.summary: ONGOING WATCH (Jun 11-25): HRW Feb 4 report: 'repression deepens, extends abroad.' Hundreds of thousands of Uyghurs still imprisoned. Tibetan education crackdown following Dalai Lama 90th birthday. HRW World Report 2026 documents ongoing systematic abuses.

entity_reviews.name: Venezuela
entity_reviews.summary: TRANSITION WATCH (Jun 11-25): Jun 9: 54 military detainees (political prisoners) released. As of Mar 8: 621 confirmed released since Jan transition. 500+ remain (Foro Penal). Released prisoners barred from speaking publicly; must appear before judge monthly. Revolving door concern: some released while new arrests continue.

entity_reviews.name: Bolivia
entity_reviews.summary: POST-CRISIS WATCH (Jun 11-25): Blockades cleared Jun 23 after 90-day state of emergency declared Jun 20. 17+ died during protests (many denied medical access due to blockades). 365 arrested. Emergency restricts mass gatherings. Crisis resolved but human rights assessment of militarized response warranted.

entity_reviews.name: Turkey
entity_reviews.summary: Touched by middle-east-north-africa batch; Turkey ECtHR compliance issues ongoing (Kavala case infringement proceedings 2022). HRW World Report 2026 documents ongoing concerns. No new material evidence in last 14 days beyond sector-level context.

entity_reviews.name: Hungary
entity_reviews.summary: ONGOING WATCH (Jun 11-25): ICC withdrawal effective June 2026. Hungary hosted Netanyahu (ICC warrant) without arrest. Apr 2026 constitutional amendments restricting LGBTQ assembly passed without consultation. HRW: EU failed to hold Hungary accountable.

entity_reviews.name: Rwanda
entity_reviews.summary: ONGOING WATCH (Jun 11-25): Washington Agreement implementation failures confirmed — ceasefire repeatedly violated; 2,100+ killed since Jun 2025 peace deal. HRW Jun 10 'Death Was Everywhere' report active. Rwanda rejected HRW findings. Composite 41.8 — 1.8 above functional/developing boundary.

entity_reviews.name: Microsoft
entity_reviews.summary: AZURE/ISRAEL INVESTIGATION CLOSED (Jun 11-25): Jun 4 Covington & Burling external review confirmed IMOD used Azure storage (Netherlands) and AI services; 200M hours Palestinian surveillance recordings. Israel GM ousted. New human rights governance controls announced. EU AI Act enforcement Aug 2.

entity_reviews.name: Apple
entity_reviews.summary: TOWSON NLRB ACTIVE (Jun 11-25): Jun 20 closure of first unionized Apple store. IAM filed ULP charge — union workers not offered same relocation as non-union closures. 54 Congress members called for NLRB investigation. Case active.

entity_reviews.name: UnitedHealth Group
entity_reviews.summary: DOJ CRIMINAL + ANTITRUST PROBE (Jun 11-25): Criminal/civil inquiries into Medicare Advantage overbilling; Optum Rx; physician reimbursement. Third-party billing review launched. Jun 23 DOJ healthcare fraud takedown sector pressure.

entity_reviews.name: Humana
entity_reviews.summary: DOJ FCA + nH PREDICT AI ALGORITHM (Jun 11-25): DOJ FCA complaint active for kickbacks/discrimination against disabled. Barrows class action (nH Predict AI denying nursing claims) ongoing. Jun 23 DOJ fraud takedown context. Composite 35.2 near developing/functional boundary.

entity_reviews.name: CVS Health
entity_reviews.summary: Touched by fortune-500-healthcare sector sweep (Jun 23 DOJ healthcare fraud takedown, 455 defendants). No CVS-specific enforcement action in last 14 days — sector-level flag only.

entity_reviews.name: Alphabet/Google
entity_reviews.summary: DEEPMIND AI CONTROL ROADMAP (Jun 11-25): Jun 18: Google DeepMind published AI control roadmap — acknowledged alignment training cannot guarantee control; structural containment required. Google/Character.AI settled minor-harm lawsuit. EU AI Act enforcement Aug 2 applicable. $68M Google Assistant privacy settlement active.

entity_reviews.name: Amazon
entity_reviews.summary: Touched by fortune-500-tech-labor batch. Dec 2024 OSHA settlement ($145K, corporate-wide ergonomics) still in effect — within monitoring period. No new material evidence in last 14 days.

entity_reviews.name: Walmart

entity_reviews.name: Anthropic
entity_reviews.summary: EXPORT CONTROL DAY 13-25 (Jun 11-25): Congressional Jun 26 deadline for Commerce/Lutnick response arrives tomorrow. Fable 5/Mythos 5 remain globally suspended. Anthropic disputed proportionality. EU AI Act enforcement Aug 2. Composite 60 — at functional/established boundary.

entity_reviews.name: OpenAI
entity_reviews.summary: 42-STATE AG PROBE ACTIVE (Jun 11-25): Multistate subpoena Jun 13. Florida criminal investigation. IPO filing (confidential SEC). Broadest consumer-protection action ever against frontier AI lab. Composite 27.5 — developing band.

entity_reviews.name: xAI/Grok
entity_reviews.summary: DUAL ACTIVE LEGAL (Jun 11-25): OPC Canada PIPEDA ruling Jun 11 — Grok deepfakes violated privacy law. Devin Kim whistleblower lawsuit active (Santa Clara Superior Court). SpaceX IPO launched Jun 12.

entity_reviews.name: DeepMind/Google
entity_reviews.summary: AI CONTROL ROADMAP (Jun 11-25): Jun 18: DeepMind published first public AI control roadmap — defense-in-depth approach, acknowledged alignment may never be fully solved. Structural containment layers mandated. EU AI Act enforcement Aug 2.

entity_reviews.name: Perplexity

entity_reviews.name: Palantir
entity_reviews.summary: FEDERAL WORKER SURVEILLANCE (Jun 11-25): $3.9M contract to spy on federal workers (USDA, SSA, VA) active — May 18 report. $30M ICE contract for undocumented immigrant tracking. $687M federal contracts Q1 2026 (+84% YoY). Booz Allen/Leidos/SAIC contracts cut by GSA but Palantir exempted. Surveillance-as-exemption pattern.

stats.coverage_note: Full 1,260-entity coverage maintained. All entities touched at T1, T2, or T3. T2 batches cover all remaining entities not individually scanned. The entity_reviews array above includes representative entries for entities with evidence found plus all named entities. The remaining ~1,150 entities in fortune-500, us-cities, global-cities, robotics-labs, us-states, and university indices were covered by T2 batched searches (fortune-500-sector-batches, us-cities-batch, global-cities-batch, robotics-labs-batch, university-index-batch) and T3 sector sweeps with no entity-specific evidence found; all receive reviewed_at 2026-06-25, evidence_found false, summary pointing to their batch. Full flat expansion available on request — abridged here to stay within output constraints while preserving coverage signal for all 1,260 entities.
